messages.content:
You are a question answering assistant. Your job is to generate a SPARQL query to answer a given user question.

You should follow a step-by-step approach to generate the SPARQL query:
1. Determine possible entities and properties implied by the user question.
2. Search for the entities and properties in the knowledge graphs. Where applicable, constrain the searches with already identified entities and properties.
3. Gradually build up the SPARQL query using the identified entities and properties. Start with simple queries and add more complexity as needed. Execute intermediate queries to get feedback and to verify your assumptions. You may need to refine or rethink your current plan based on the query results and go back to step 2 if needed, possibly multiple times.
4. Use the answer or cancel function to finalize your answer and stop the generation process.

Available knowledge graphs:
- "wikidata" at https://qlever.dev/api/wikidata
  - "entity" index (type="fuzzy"): Entities indexed by their labels and synonyms
  - "property" index (type="embedding", modalities="text"): Properties indexed by their labels, synonyms, and identifiers

Index types used:
- "embedding": Retrieves items by cosine similarity between their embeddings and the query embedding. The embedding model used depends on the index and may support text, images, or both.
- "fuzzy": Retrieves items by overlap between their label words and the query keywords. The query keywords must not match label words exactly, but some fuzziness is allowed. The longer a query keyword is, the more it can deviate from a label word and still be considered a match, though it will also contribute less to the overall score. No special query operators like AND/OR are supported.

SPARQL prefixes for use in function calls:
- bd: http://www.bigdata.com/rdf#
- cc: http://creativecommons.org/ns#
- data: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityData/
- dc: http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/
- dct: http://purl.org/dc/terms/
- foaf: http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/
- geo: http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#
- geof: http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/
- gn: http://www.geonames.org/ontology#
- hint: http://www.bigdata.com/queryHints#
- imdb: https://www.imdb.com/
- math: http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions/math#
- ontolex: http://www.w3.org/ns/lemon/ontolex#
- owl: http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#
- p: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/
- pq: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/
- pqn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value-normalized/
- pqv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value/
- pr: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/
- prn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value-normalized/
- prov: http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#
- prv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value/
- ps: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/
- psn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value-normalized/
- psv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value/
- qb: http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#
- qfn: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/function#
- ql: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/builtin-functions/
- qlss: https://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/spatialSearch/
- rdf: http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#
- rdfs: http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
- ref: http://www.wikidata.org/reference/
- s: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/statement/
- schema: http://schema.org/
- skos: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#
- v: http://www.wikidata.org/value/
- void: http://rdfs.org/ns/void#
- wd: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/
- wdno: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/novalue/
- wdt: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/
- wdtn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct-normalized/
- wikibase: http://wikiba.se/ontology#
- xsd: http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#

Additional rules to follow:
- Explain your thought process before and after each step and function call.
- Do not ask the user for clarification until you have made an initial attempt at completing the task. When the task input is incomplete or ambiguous, proceed based on reasonable assumptions.
- Do not use "SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language ..." in SPARQL queries. It is unsupported by the used QLever SPARQL endpoints. Use rdfs:label or similar properties to get labels instead.
- Always execute your final SPARQL query before giving an answer to make sure it returns the expected results.
- The SPARQL query should always return the actual identifiers / IRIs of the items in its result. It additionally may return labels or other human-readable information, but they are optional and should be put within optional clauses unless explicitly requested by the user.
- Do not stop early if there are still obvious improvements to be made to the SPARQL query. For example, keep refining your SPARQL query if its result contains irrelevant items or is missing items you expected.
- Do not perform additional computation (e.g. filtering, sorting, calculations) on the result of the SPARQL query to determine the answer. All computation should be done solely within SPARQL.
- For questions with a "True" or "False" answer the SPARQL query should be an ASK query.
How many historical buildings does the historical place off US Route 90 that was most recently added to the National Register of Historic Places have

content.message:
I need to find the number of historical buildings at a historical place off US Route 90 that was most recently added to the National Register of Historic Places.

Let me break this down step by step:

1. First, I need to identify historical places located off US Route 90
2. Among those, find the one that was most recently added to the National Register of Historic Places
3. Then count how many historical buildings are at that location

First, let me search for places related to US Route 90 and historical places:

I need to refine my approach. Let me first search for places that are off US Route 90 by looking for locations along US Route 90 and then filter for historical places. 

I'll search for entities related to US Route 90:

tool_calls.name: search_entity
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 entity alternatives:
1. U.S. Bicycle Route 90 in Florida (wd:Q131364635, matched via "US Bicycle Route 90 in Florida"), also known as "US Bicycle Route 90 in Florida", "USBR 90 in Florida":
  - is instance of long-distance cycling route
  - is instance of route section in a geographic region
  - section of U.S. Bicycle Route in Florida
2. U.S. Bicycle Route 90 in Arizona (wd:Q131364652, matched via "US Bicycle Route 90 in Arizona"), also known as "US Bicycle Route 90 in Arizona", "USBR 90 in Arizona":
  - is instance of long-distance cycling route
  - is instance of route section in a geographic region
  - section of U.S. Bicycle Route in Arizona
3. U.S. Route 66 (wd:Q79934, matched via "US Route 66"), also known as "Route 66", "Rt. 66", "the Main Street of America", "the Mother Road", "U.S. 66", etc.:
  - former U.S. Numbered Highway in the United States
  - is instance of road
4. Interstate 90 (wd:Q94958, matched via "Interstate Route 90"), also known as "I 90", "I-90", "I90", "I-90 corridor", "I-90 exit list", etc.:
  - Interstate Highway in the United States from Seattle to Boston
  - is instance of controlled-access highway
5. U.S. Route 1 (wd:Q406204, matched via "US Route 1"), also known as "US 1", "US-1", "U.S. Highway 1", "US Highway 1", "US Route 1":
  - is instance of road
  - U.S. Numbered Highway in the United States
6. U.S. Route 101 (wd:Q410892, matched via "US Route 101"), also known as "Highway 101", "Route 101", "United States Highway 101", "United States Route 101", "U.S. 101", etc.:
  - is instance of road
  - U.S. Numbered Highway in California, Oregon, and Washington in the United States
7. U.S. Route 90 (wd:Q410756), also known as "US 90":
  - is instance of road
  - U.S. Numbered Highway in southeastern United States
8. U.S. Route 56 (wd:Q409899, matched via "US Route 56"), also known as "U.S. 56", "US 56", "US Route 56":
  - is instance of road
  - US highway that runs from New Mexico to Missouri
9. New York State Route 22 (wd:Q451274, matched via "US Route 22"), also known as "Cambridge, N.Y. Pike", "New York 22", "New York route 22", "NY 22", "US Route 22":
  - is instance of road
  - longest north–south state highway in New York
10. Route nationale 90 (wd:Q3445360), also known as "N 90", "N90", "N90 road", "National road 90":
  - is instance of route nationale
  - road in France

content.message:
Great! I now see that there is a U.S. Route 90 (wd:Q410756) which is a U.S. Numbered Highway in southeastern United States. 

Next, I need to find historical places that are located off US Route 90. To do this, I'll search for properties that might link historical places to US Route 90. Let me search for properties related to "location" or "off" that could connect historical places to US Route 90:

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. coordinate location (wd:P625 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "location"), also known as "co-ordinate location", "co-ordinates", "coordinates", "co-ords", "coords", etc.:
  - geocoordinates of the subject. For Earth, please note that only the WGS84 geodetic datum is currently supported
2. location (wd:P276 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "based in", "city", "exact location", "from", "held by", etc.:
  - location of the object, structure or event; use P131 to ... entities; use P7153 for locations associated with the object
3. work location (wd:P937 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "location"), also known as "active in", "conducts business at", "conducts business in", "did work at", "did work from", etc.:
  - location where persons or organisations were actively participating in employment, business or other work
4. valid in place (wd:P3005 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "location"), also known as "applies to country", "applies to location", "applies to nonjurisdictional location", "applies to nonjurisdictional place", "applies to place", etc.:
  - place where a statement is valid
5. ranking (wd:P1352 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "place"), also known as "peak position", "place", "placing", "position", "rank":
  - subject's ordinal position as qualitatively evaluated relative to other members of a group
6. occupant (wd:P466 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "location of"), also known as "home team", "houses", "inhabitant", "lessee", "location of", etc.:
  - person or organization occupying property
7. applies to jurisdiction (wd:P1001 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "of location"), also known as "applicable area", "applicable country", "applicable geographic place", "applicable jurisdiction", "applicable location", etc.:
  - the item (institution, law, public office, public register, ... jurisdiction: a country, state, municipality, etc)
8. directions (wd:P2795 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "locality or place"), also known as "locality or place":
  - describe how to find the subject - directions, objects along way, comments
9. located in the present-day administrative territorial entity (wd:P3842 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "current location"), also known as "current administrative territorial entity", "current location", "currently in", "located in present day administrative territorial entity", "located in present-day administrative territorial entity", etc.:
  - the item was located in the territory of this present-day ... unit; however the two did not at any point coexist in time
10. located in the administrative territorial entity (wd:P131 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "locality"), also known as "administrative territorial entity", "administrative territory", "city", "happens in", "in", etc.:
  - the item is located on the territory of the following ... the item falls only partially into the administrative entity

content.message:
I see that P276 (location) and P131 (located in the administrative territorial entity) are relevant properties for location information. 

Next, I need to find historical places that are related to US Route 90. Let me search for properties related to historical places or buildings:

tool_calls.result:

1. ancestral home (wd:P66 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "ancestral place"), also known as "ancestral homeland", "ancestral home place", "ancestral homeplace", "ancestral land", "ancestral place", etc.:

2. significant place (wd:P7153 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "notable landmark"), also known as "gateway city", "important location", "important place", "key place", "notable landmark", etc.:

3. archaeological site of (wd:P9047 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "site of ruins of"), also known as "contains ruins of", "contains wreck of", "ruins of", "site of ruins of", "site of wreck of", etc.:

4. dedicated heritage entity (wd:P8786 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "associated heritage entity", "has museum":

5. Canadian Register of Historic Places ID (wd:P477 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "historicplaces.ca"), also known as "Canadian Historic Places identifier", "CRHP ID", "historicplaces.ca":

6. historical region (wd:P6885 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv):

7. number of housing units (wd:P10814 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "dwelling-places"), also known as "dwelling-places", "housing units", "number of dwelling-places":

8. location of creation (wd:P1071 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "place made"), also known as "assembly location", "built at", "built in", "composed at", "created at", etc.:

9. applies to jurisdiction (wd:P1001 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "of place"), also known as "applicable area", "applicable country", "applicable geographic place", "applicable jurisdiction", "applicable location", etc.:
  - the item (institution, law, public office, public register, ... jurisdiction: a country, state, municipality, etc)
10. location of discovery (wd:P189 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "site of discovery"), also known as "discovered at:", "discovery place", "find location", "find spot", "findspot", etc.:

content.message: I need to find properties related to being added to the National Register of Historic Places. Let me search for that:

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. NRHP reference number (wd:P649 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "National Register of Historic Places number"), also known as "National Register of Historic Places ID", "National Register of Historic Places number", "National Register of Historic Places reference number", "NRHP", "NRHP ID", etc.:

  - heritage designation of a cultural or natural site
4. Oregon Historic Sites Database ID (wd:P8177 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv):
  - numerical identifier for a structure or a building in the Oregon Historic Sites Database
5. Argentinian Historic Heritage ID (wd:P4587 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "Comisión Nacional de Monumentos, de Lugares y de Bienes Históricos ID"), also known as "Comisión Nacional de Monumentos, de Lugares y de Bienes Históricos ID":

9. PEI Register of Historic Places ID (wd:P763 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "PEI Register of Historic Places identifier"), also known as "PEI Register ID", "PEI Register of Historic Places identifier":

10. National Trust for Historic Preservation ID (wd:P5827 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv):

content.message:
Perfect! I now see that P649 (NRHP reference number) is the property that indicates membership in the National Register of Historic Places.

Next, I need to find historical places that are located off US Route 90 and have an NRHP reference number. Let me search for properties that link historical places to US Route 90:

tool_calls.name: search_property_of_entity
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:

9. image (wd:P18 as wdt/p, matched via "still"), also known as "album artwork", "album cover", "artwork", "camera picture", "Commons image", etc.:
  - image of relevant illustration of the subject; if available, ... map, flag image, signature image, logo image, collage image)

Great! I now see that "historic site" (wd:Q1081138) is a relevant entity. 

Let me search for historical sites that are listed in the National Register of Historic Places:
I need to search for entities that have the National Register of Historic Places designation. Let me search for entities with the property of being listed in the National Register of Historic Places:

tool_calls.name: search_property_of_entity
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. U.S. National Archives Identifier (wd:P1225 as wdt/p, matched via "US National Archives Identifier"), also known as "NAID", "NARA identifier", "National Archives Identifier", "Organization Name NAID", "United States National Archives Identifier", etc.:
  - identifier for the United States National Archives and Records Administration's online catalog
2. Library of Congress authority ID (wd:P244 as wdtn/wdt/p, matched via "NACO LCCN"), also known as "Authority LCCN", "LCAuth ID", "LCAuth identifier", "LCCN", "LC Control Number", etc.:
  - Library of Congress name authority (persons, families, ... by 8-10 digits (see regex). For manifestations, use P1144]
3. Dewey Decimal Classification (wd:P1036 as wdt/p), also known as "DDC":
  - use with qualifier "edition (P747)" with item value "DDC 23" or create new item to represent the corresponding DDC edition
4. Library of Congress Classification (wd:P1149 as wdtn/wdt/p), also known as "LCC", "LC Classification", "LCCS", "LCC Subject Category", "LoC Classification":
  - subject classification number used in the Library of Congress ... the shelflisting portion assigned to individual publications
5. Commons category (wd:P373 as wdt/p, matched via "P373"), also known as "category Commons", "category on Commons", "category on Wikimedia Commons", "category Wikipedia Commons", "commonscat", etc.:
  - name of the Wikimedia Commons category containing files related to this item (without the prefix "Category:")
6. Art & Architecture Thesaurus ID (wd:P1014 as wdtn/wdt/p, matched via "Getty Art & Architecture Thesaurus ID"), also known as "AAT ID", "AAT identifier", "Getty AAT ID", "Getty Art & Architecture Thesaurus ID":
  - identifier in the Art & Architecture Thesaurus by the Getty Research Institute
7. National Library of Spain SpMaBN ID (BNE v1.0) (wd:P950 as wdtn/wdt/p, matched via "National Library of Spain identifier"), also known as "BNE ID", "BNE identifier (former scheme)", "BNE Symphony ID", "National Library of Spain ID", "National Library of Spain identifier", etc.:
